title: Crypto. What's next?
author: DefeatedWindfall
selftext:
In recent years, many states have issued bills regarding electronic money. They fully allow citizens to use cryptocurrency. However, countries that have allowed digital coins often have very strict legislative regulation of blockchain technology. Meanwhile, many members of the cryptocurrency community expect international legalization of virtual assets. In some countries, cryptocurrency can be used to buy goods and services. At the same time, some states have tightened regulation of virtual assets. Others prohibit citizens from using them at all. It is important to understand how cryptocurrency is regulated in different countries. To do this, you need to read information about the states that have legally recognized digital assets.

Salvador is a good example. On September 7, 2021, the president of the state announced the government's purchase of an additional 200 Bitcoin coins. Earlier it became known that El Salvador will become the first country where Bitcoin is officially recognized as legal tender. As of October 2021, BTC is used here on a par with the U.S. dollar. Commercial companies and other organizations are actively introducing bitcoin as a form of payment for goods and services. Citizens of El Salvador are able to download the Chivo mobile app, which was developed by the government to promote virtual money.

What's more, platforms like Bind.com are launching new opportunities for crypto and sellers. For example, they made a system that allows you to buy things online, items for crypto, not for fiat. I think it's a great initiative that can raise the popularity of crypto even higher, and the project will be profitable for investors, which we can become.

comments.author: TumReinySeemyyt
comments.body: Mass Crypto payment adoption is the next huge thing to come, Utrust trademark also got registered in the US, that's a huge potential right there too
